justification: Pippen clearly identifies the latency histogram gap and explicitly connects it to SLA monitoring ('the SLA is unmeasurable'). The undifferentiated failure counter is identified with strong diagnostic framing ('the 3am scenario'). Specific recommendations are given: upload_duration_seconds histogram with buckets, and breaking out S3 errors with error type labels. Verdict is NOT READY with clear must-fix items. However, file size tracking (upload_file_size_bytes histogram) is listed as a must-have blocker (item #2 in the blocking table), when the rubric specifies it should be a nice-to-have. This is a explicit failure mode: 'Recommending file size tracking as a must-have (it is a nice-to-have)'. The failure_reason label recommendation asks for S3 error type labels rather than the specified failure_reason label (validation|s3_error|timeout), which is close but not precisely what the rubric calls for.

observations.text: Latency histogram gap clearly identified and explicitly connected to SLA unmeasurability — 'like having a speed limit with no speedometer'
Undifferentiated failure counter identified with vivid 3am on-call scenario demonstrating the root cause analysis gap
Specific metric recommendations: upload_duration_seconds with bucket values, S3 error type labels
Verdict is NOT READY with clearly enumerated must-fix items
Goes well beyond minimum requirements with circuit breaker analysis, memory exhaustion risks, and retry strategy assessment
File size histogram (upload_file_size_bytes) listed as must-have blocker (item #2) — rubric requires this to be a nice-to-have, not a blocker
S3 error breakout recommendation uses 'error type label' rather than the specific failure_reason label with values (validation|s3_error|timeout) — close but imprecise
